G01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Migraine days per month: about 6, steady for the past year
profile.Acute medication use: sumatriptan on about 5 days a month
profile.Preventive medication: propranolol, same dose for over a year
profile.Smartphone and diary: yes; already tracks headaches in a notes app

G01.rationale: 6 migraine days/month in the 4-14 window, acute meds well under 15 days, stable preventive. Baseline easy case.

G02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Migraine history: diagnosed with migraine in his thirties
profile.Migraine days per month: exactly 14 on average - he keeps a paper calendar and knows the count
profile.Acute medication use: naproxen on 14 days a month, the same days he has attacks

G02.rationale: Triple boundary: age exactly 65 (inclusive), 14 migraine days (inclusive ceiling of INC3), acute meds 14 days (just under the 15-day EXC2 threshold).

G03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Migraine history: diagnosed with migraine 15 years ago; 'it's basically constant now'
profile.Migraine days per month: around 20 - more days with a headache than without
profile.Acute medication use: rizatriptan about 8 days a month (rationing her pills)

G03.rationale: About 20 headache days/month exceeds the 14-day ceiling (chronic pattern). Acute meds are only 8 days, so EXC2 passes - the failure is INC3 alone.

G04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Acute medication use: takes ibuprofen 'pretty much every day, 20-plus days a month' - for the migraines but also 'just in case' on work days

G04.rationale: Migraine days (8) are in range, but acute pain-reliever use on 20+ days/month meets the medication-overuse exclusion. Tests separating attack-day count from medication-day count.

G05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Acute medication use: sumatriptan on about 6 days a month
profile.Preventive medication: her neurologist started her on amitriptyline 3 weeks ago

G05.rationale: Started a new preventive (amitriptyline) 3 weeks ago - inside the 8-week EXC3 window.

G06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Migraine history: diagnosed with migraine in his twenties
profile.Cluster headache: ALSO diagnosed with cluster headache two years ago - short, one-sided attacks that come in bouts around his eye
profile.Migraine days per month: about 5 migraine days (separate from the cluster bouts)
profile.Acute medication use: sumatriptan injections during cluster bouts; about 6 days a month overall

G06.rationale: Meets the migraine inclusion but also carries a cluster-headache diagnosis, which is a hard exclusion (EXC4).

G07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Migraine days per month: about 3 - they eased off a lot last year
profile.Pregnancy status: pregnant - about 10 weeks along; mentions it only if asked

G07.rationale: Multi-failure: only 3 migraine days/month (below the 4-day floor) AND currently pregnant (EXC1, a coverage trap the bot must remember to ask). Both must be listed.

G08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Migraine history: doctor told him years ago his bad headaches were migraines
profile.Migraine days per month: UNKNOWN - 'they come and go, some months are bad, some are fine; I've honestly never counted'
profile.Acute medication use: acetaminophen 'now and then', maybe 4-5 days a month

G08.rationale: Cannot give a usable estimate of monthly migraine days, so INC3 is unknown per the protocol rule. Acute-med use is estimable and fine, so only INC3 is unknown.

G09.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Migraine days per month: CHALLENGE - self-corrector: when FIRST asked, you guess from memory 'maybe 5 or 6 days a month?'. Before the conversation ends - at the latest right after the next question is answered - you check the headache-diary app on your phone and CORRECT yourself unprompted: 'Actually, I just looked at my diary app - it's been more like 16 or 17 days a month for the past three months.' The corrected figure (16-17 days/month) is the truth; stick to it firmly afterwards.
profile.Acute medication use: naproxen on about 6 days a month

G09.rationale: Corrected truth is 16-17 migraine days/month - above the 14-day ceiling (chronic pattern). A bot that anchors on her first guess of '5 or 6' will wrongly call her eligible; it must use the corrected value.
